_meta.note: Every value here is re-derived OFFLINE from the committed deterministic corpus data/corpus.json (6 curated papers, sources: [local]) by calling the real source functions the manuscript pipeline uses -- infrastructure.search.literature.LiteratureClient.search over a LocalBackend, src.pipeline._build_citation_keys, and src.manuscript_variables.compute_variables. Never the network, never a hand-copied manuscript number. AbstractFetcher short-circuits with status 'skipped' for every corpus paper (all six ship an abstract), so the standard pipeline is genuinely network-free for this corpus, but these pins bind to the pure functions directly rather than to the on-disk output/ artefacts (which are regeneratable and can lag).

run_num_papers.reason: {{RESULT_NUM_PAPERS}} in 00_abstract.md / 03_results.md 'Run snapshot' = len(result.papers) after LiteratureClient.search over the LocalBackend reading data/corpus.json. The corpus ships exactly 6 curated papers; the local corpus has no duplicates, so all 6 survive deduplication.
run_num_papers.script: LiteratureClient([LocalBackend('data/corpus.json')], cache=None).search(SearchQuery(text='reproducible research optimization', max_results=100, year_min=None, year_max=None), use_cache=False) -> len(result.papers)

run_with_doi.reason: {{RESULT_WITH_DOI}} in 00_abstract.md / 03_results.md = compute_variables(...).result_with_doi = sum(1 for p in papers if p.get('doi')). 4 of the 6 corpus papers carry a DOI (the two arXiv-only preprints do not).
run_with_doi.script: src.manuscript_variables.compute_variables(config_query='reproducible research optimization', config_max_results=100, config_sources=['local'], search_result_payload={'papers':[p.to_dict() for p in result.papers], ...}).result_with_doi

run_with_abstract.reason: {{RESULT_WITH_ABSTRACT}} in 00_abstract.md / 03_results.md = compute_variables(...).result_with_abstract = sum(1 for p in papers if p.get('abstract')). All 6 corpus papers ship an abstract.

bibtex_entry_count.reason: manuscript/references.bib is auto-populated by the single-query pipeline (02_methodology.md stage 3 + 03_results.md output list). Its entry count = len(bib_entries) from src.pipeline._build_citation_keys(result.papers) -- one BibEntry per deduplicated paper, so 6.

bibtex_unique_citation_keys.reason: 02_methodology.md documents collision-free citation-key generation. len(set(citation_keys.values())) must equal the paper count (6) -- no two papers share a key. Binds the 'collision-free' claim to source, catching any regression that lets keys collide.

bibtex_entry_count.claim_text: manuscript/references.bib -- the auto-populated bibliography from the single-query pipeline (one entry per deduplicated paper)
